title: parapolystichum currorii
intro: Parapolystichum currorii, also known as the Curror's fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical forests of Madagascar, belonging to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. Its health benefits include treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as reducing fever and soothing skin irritations. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions for its medicinal uses, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy.
